skill.name: data-visualization-tool
skill.description: Creates chart guidance for DBX Studio by selecting chart types, drafting SQL data queries, and defining labels for clear visualizations.
skill.summary: Plan clear DBX Studio charts with suitable chart types, SQL data queries, and readable labels.
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, SQL snippets, and weak-crypto keywords, but review found no executable scripts or malicious intent. The skill is documentation-only and describes chart selection, SQL query patterns, and visualization labels. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, filesystem access, or command execution was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Syntax
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the lines contain Markdown inline code, chart type names, SQL examples, and fenced code blocks. No executable shell or Ruby code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is inside Markdown documentation and SQL examples. There is no command runner, script file, or instruction to execute the backticked text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the matching text is ordinary prose or SQL ordering syntax. No cryptographic API, hashing function, or encryption routine appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows descriptive text and SQL sort direction, not DES, MD5, SHA1, or similar security code. No security-sensitive data processing is implemented.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance, but the referenced line advises aggregating large result sets before charting. It does not request host, user, process, network, or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a data visualization best practice about avoiding raw row-level data. No reconnaissance command or system information collection is present.

content.value_statement: Raw database results can be hard to interpret and share. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code plan charts with suitable data queries and readable labels.
actual_capabilities: Selects chart types for comparison, trend, share, distribution, and correlation questions.
Drafts SQL query patterns for bar, line, and pie chart datasets.
Defines chart configuration fields such as title, x axis, y axis, and grouping.
Encourages aggregation before charting large datasets.
Applies simple design rules for readable titles and axis labels.
limitations: Does not include executable code for rendering charts outside DBX Studio.
Requires the user or connected tool to provide valid database schema context.
Only documents a small set of common chart types.
Does not validate SQL safety, permissions, or database performance.

use_cases.title: Build an Analytics Chart
use_cases.description: Create a chart plan that compares categories, shows trends, or summarizes status counts.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Dashboard Question
use_cases.description: Turn a business question into a chart type, query shape, and readable axis labels.

use_cases.title: Standardize Visualization Guidance
use_cases.description: Use consistent chart selection rules when generating DBX Studio visualizations for users.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Chart Type
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to visualize this data question: [question]. Choose the best chart type and explain the x axis, y axis, and title.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Query and Chart Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DBX Studio chart plan for [business question]. Use these tables and columns: [schema]. Include the chart type, data query goal, title, and grouping.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Visualization
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate refinement of a chart request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this chart idea: [chart details]. Improve the chart type, aggregation, title, and axis labels so the result is easier to understand.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multi-Chart Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for a broader analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a set of DBX Studio charts for this analysis: [analysis goal]. For each chart, define the question answered, chart type, query pattern, grouping, and labeling guidance.

output_examples.input: Show monthly revenue by product category.
output_examples.output: A grouped bar or line chart is recommended, depending on whether category comparison or time trend is more important. The chart should use month on the x axis, revenue on the y axis, and product category as the grouping.

output_examples.input: Show the share of orders by status.
output_examples.output: A pie chart is suitable if there are seven or fewer statuses. The result should group small statuses into Other and use a title that names the order population.

output_examples.input: Find whether discount size relates to order value.
output_examples.output: A scatter plot is recommended. Use discount size on one axis and order value on the other, then check for clustering or visible correlation.

best_practices: Aggregate large datasets before charting to keep results readable and efficient.
Use human-readable axis labels instead of raw database column names.
Choose pie charts only for small part-to-whole comparisons.
anti_patterns: Do not chart raw row-level data when an aggregated result answers the question.
Do not use pie charts for many categories or precise comparisons.
Do not choose a chart before clarifying the business question and available fields.

faq.question: What chart types does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers bar, line, pie, scatter, area, and histogram chart guidance.

faq.question: Does this skill render charts directly?
faq.answer: No. It describes how to configure charts for DBX Studio and related workflows.

faq.question: Can it write SQL for chart data?
faq.answer: It provides SQL query patterns, but users should validate the query against their database schema and permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive data?
faq.answer: The skill does not collect or transmit data, but users should avoid exposing sensitive records in chart prompts.

faq.question: Can it help with dashboard design?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan chart choices, labels, grouping, and aggregation for dashboard questions.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
